codeReview.summary: This feature was a verification-only integration pass. The single commit updates dashboard/data/frontend_dashboard.json with real streetwear pipeline output (7 directions, 21 evidence entries, 4 product lines). No code changes were made — the feature's purpose was end-to-end validation of all 5 dashboard pages with real data and new visual charts. The regenerated JSON has correct schema (all required top-level and nested fields present), valid cross-references (evidence→directions, product_lines→directions, fourteen_day_changes→directions), correct judgment counts matching direction count (0+0+3+4=7), and all movement_history arrays have exactly 14 elements. The worker performed thorough browser-based verification covering all expectedBehavior items: bubble navigation (2 directions), evidence card navigation (3 cards), chart rendering across all pages, Chinese text consistency, and 0 console errors. All 138 tests pass.

issues.description: All 7 directions have identical boilerplate text patterns: descriptions follow '{name}趋势方向的核心特征和受众定位', why_it_matters follows '{name}在最近数据中显示出稳定的趋势特征，值得关注。', content_cues are identical across all directions, and risks all have the same '市场饱和风险/差异化定位' entry. This is a pipeline output quality issue (from frontend_builder.py), not a bug in this verification feature, but worth noting for future improvement.

All evidence entries have freshness='最近7天' regardless of their actual timestamps (some dating back to 2023-01-28). The freshness field appears to be a static default rather than computed from the timestamp. Again, this is a pipeline generation issue, not a verification bug.

No directions have judgment_state '值得跟' or '观察中' (counts are 0 for both). The verification confirms the donut chart and judgment module render correctly with these zero-count states, but the data doesn't exercise all 4 judgment paths with non-zero values. The jade_dashboard.json dataset covers this gap for backward-compat testing.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker committed regenerated frontend_dashboard.json data to the repository. AGENTS.md says 'Do NOT modify existing example outputs in examples/' but does not address whether generated data files in dashboard/data/ should be committed or git-ignored. This is a gray area — the file serves as both test fixture and default dashboard data.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Commit 5f3e5dc changes only dashboard/data/frontend_dashboard.json (790 insertions, 57 deletions). No .gitignore rule covers this file.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker's skill feedback suggests adding a cross-page integration verification checklist to the bridge-worker skill. The current skill does not have specific guidance for integration verification features — step 4 covers basic frontend verification but not multi-page navigation testing or data consistency checks.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: skillFeedback.suggestedChanges: 'Consider adding a verification checklist to the bridge-worker skill for cross-page integration features that explicitly lists: (1) run pack end-to-end, (2) copy JSON to dashboard/data, (3) test bubble navigation with >=2 clicks, (4) test evidence navigation with >=3 clicks, (5) verify all chart types render, (6) check Chinese text on all pages'

summary: PASS. This verification-only feature updated frontend_dashboard.json with real streetwear pipeline output and confirmed cross-page integration across all 5 dashboard pages. The commit contains only the regenerated data file (no code changes), which is appropriate for an integration verification feature. Data schema is complete and valid with correct cross-references. Worker performed thorough browser-based testing covering all expectedBehavior items (navigation, charts, Chinese text, 0 errors). Three non-blocking observations noted about boilerplate text patterns and static freshness values in pipeline output — these are upstream data quality items, not integration bugs. All 138 tests pass.
